bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started, and the opponent has opened with an offer that gives me the absolute minimum utility, presumably maximizing their own. Given the very early stage of the negotiation (t=0.0), I will counter by offering my ideal configuration, which maximizes my own utility, to establish a strong opening position.

The negotiation has just started (t=0.02), but the opponent has already made a significant concession on issueA and issueB while holding firm on issueC and issueD. This suggests they value issueC and issueD more heavily. Since issueA and issueB are my most important issues, I will hold firm on them and make a small initial concession on my lowest-priority issue, issueD, to reciprocate their movement.

The negotiation is still in the very early stages (t=0.04), giving me ample time to explore tradeoffs without dropping my utility significantly. The opponent consistently insists on valueF for issueC and issueD, which are my lower priority issues (weights 0.2 and 0.1 respectively). I will maintain my highest utility requests for issues A and B while slightly conceding further on issue C and maintaining my previous concession on issue D, keeping my utility around 0.88.

This creates an offer with a strong utility for me (0.76) while taking a clear step toward their stated position.
